openai.raw:
Independent assessments

Image 1 — CONTROL
- Color/transparency: Pale yellow core; overall semi-transparent with a slight green-gray surrounding haze.
- Structures: Clear, cohesive gel-like mass adhering to the wall; thin peripheral film/meniscus; no long fibrin strands visible.
- Stage: Consistent with a formed clot mass (partial_clot).
- Density/homogeneity: Dense, smooth, relatively homogeneous central body.
- Unique features: Truncated‑dome plug with a clean boundary and annular meniscus along the container wall.

Image 2 — CH19 (time acceleration)
- Color/transparency: Pale-to-medium yellow; surrounding zone appears more turbid.
- Structures: Cohesive clot with visible internal veils/sheets suggestive of fibrin organization; surrounding matrix shows fine cloudiness.
- Stage: Most advanced of the three; consistent with full_coagulation.
- Density/homogeneity: Higher apparent density and heterogeneity due to internal fibrin veils.
- Unique features: Lamellar/veiled textures within the clot; broader turbid halo.

Image 3 — CH21 (time deceleration)
- Color/transparency: Pale yellow nucleus within a larger, clearer grayish dome; comparatively more transparent.
- Structures: Central compact clot in each aliquot; minimal visible fibrin strands in the periphery.
- Stage: Partial_clot.
- Density/homogeneity: Moderately dense but smoother and more homogeneous than CH19.
- Unique features: Core–shell morphology (yellow nucleus encased by clearer gel); three similar domes along the dish rim.

Comparisons
- Most advanced coagulation: CH19.
- Least advanced coagulation: CH21.
- Control vs treated: Visible differences present. CH19 shows increased opacity/turbidity and internal fibrin veils; CH21 appears clearer with a smaller, more defined central nucleus.
- CH19 vs CH21: CH19 is more opaque and fibrin-rich; CH21 is more transparent with smoother morphology and less peripheral fibrin.
- Overall visual difference between control and treated: Moderate.

openai.raw:
Per-sample observations

CONTROL (Image 1)
- Color/transparency: Pale yellow; overall semi-transparent.
- Coagulation structures: No obvious fibrin strands or clots; smooth, uniform liquid meniscus. A thin whitish rim/film along the glass at the contact line.
- Stage: none.
- Density/homogeneity: Homogeneous fluid; no visible particulates or networks.
- Unique features: Clean central droplet with smooth edges; faint peripheral film on glass.

CH19 — time acceleration (Image 2)
- Color/transparency: Pale yellow core with an extended grayish/yellow translucent film.
- Coagulation structures: Yes — central gel-like mass; surrounding extended fibrin-like sheet with dense reticular lines; polygonal “crazing” cracks and radial striations; numerous small dot-like inclusions.
- Stage: partial_clot.
- Density/homogeneity: Markedly heterogeneous; dense center grading to thinner film; discontinuities/cracks across the film.
- Unique features: Broad reticulated sheet with polygonal crack network; radially oriented fibrillar lines.

CH21 — time deceleration (Image 3)
- Color/transparency: Deeper yellow central plug with a bright yellow peripheral band; semi-transparent film elsewhere; center more opaque.
- Coagulation structures: Yes — larger, more consolidated central coagulum; thick peripheral rim/band of fibrin-like material; fine honeycomb/reticular microstructure extending outward; very dense micro-texture on one side.
- Stage: partial_clot (visibly more advanced than CH19 but not universally opaque).
- Density/homogeneity: Strongly heterogeneous; highest density at center with a continuous structured rim and fine network beyond.
- Unique features: Prominent yellow rim with cell-like compartments; finer, denser micro-reticulation than CH19.

Comparisons
- Most advanced coagulation: CH21 (largest opaque central mass, thick fibrin rim, densest network).
- Least coagulated: CONTROL (uniform liquid, no visible fibrin/clots).
- Control vs treated: Pronounced visual differences; treated samples show organized fibrin networks and central clots absent in control.
- CH19 vs CH21: Both show clotting, but CH21 has a larger, more opaque core and a thicker continuous peripheral band with finer micro-reticulation; CH19 shows broader translucent sheet with polygonal cracking and less consolidated center.

Independent assessments

Image 1 (CONTROL)
- Color/transparency: bright yellow overall with whitish opalescent zones; overall semi-transparent.
- Clots/fibrin: yes — irregular flocculent coagulum with cloudy masses; small adherent aggregates on glass; no long discrete strands visible.
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot (substantial gelled mass with residual free liquid).
- Density/homogeneity: heterogeneous; mixed dense opaque patches and clearer fluid.
- Unique features: irregular, lobulated gel adhering to tube wall; scattered small particulates.

Image 2 (CH19 — time acceleration)
- Color/transparency: yellow; overall semi-transparent tending to opaque in the core.
- Clots/fibrin: yes — cohesive spherical gel showing a fine reticular/mesh pattern consistent with a fibrin network across most of the visible mass.
- Coagulation stage: full_coagulation (nearly the entire sample volume visible is gelled with a sharp boundary; only a thin surrounding halo of fluid).
- Density/homogeneity: dense and relatively homogeneous; uniform reticulated texture.
- Unique features: pronounced honeycomb-like network across the clot surface; well-defined spherical contour.

Image 3 (CH21 — time deceleration)
- Color/transparency: yellow; overall semi-transparent with clearer supernatant.
- Clots/fibrin: yes — compact, smooth oval central coagulum; weak or no visible strand network; surrounding clearer liquid.
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot (localized dense core with remaining liquid volume).
- Density/homogeneity: central dense core with gradient to periphery; less homogeneous than CH19.
- Unique features: smooth-surfaced, well-contained core clot with a circumferential clear zone.

Comparisons
- Most advanced coagulation: CH19 (largest fraction of sample appears as a cohesive gel with a reticular network).
- Least advanced: CH21 (smallest gelled volume; smooth compact core with more surrounding liquid).
- Control vs treated: both treated samples differ from control in morphology; control shows irregular flocculent coagulum, while CH19 is a dense, uniform reticular gel and CH21 is a compact core clot.
- CH19 vs CH21: CH19 shows extensive reticular fibrin pattern and greater gelled volume; CH21 shows smoother, smaller core with less visible network.
- Overall visual difference between control and treated: pronounced.

openai.raw:
Independent assessments

CONTROL (Image 1)
- Color and transparency: Pale yellow; largely semi-opaque with only thin translucent margins.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. One large, consolidated gelatinous mass adherent to the inner wall; smooth outer boundary with faint internal reticulations.
- Coagulation stage: Partial_clot (advanced, with a dominant cohesive clot; some residual liquid likely present).
- Density and homogeneity: Dense, compact coagulum; heterogeneous overall (solid mass plus small amount of clearer fluid).
- Unique features: Disc- or plaque-like clot attached to the sidewall; rounded edges; surface looks slightly folded.

CH19 — time acceleration (Image 2)
- Color and transparency: Bright yellow; clear/transparent.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. Thin, translucent sheet/veil along the bottom curving up one side; appears as delicate fibrin film rather than a solid mass.
- Coagulation stage: Early_fibrin.
- Density and homogeneity: Mostly homogeneous liquid; localized very thin film at the bottom/side.
- Unique features: Crescent-shaped lamellar film with a single wisp extending upward.

CH21 — time deceleration (Image 3)
- Color and transparency: Bright yellow; clear/transparent.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. Broader, more continuous sheet-like membrane spanning much of the lower hemisphere with multiple folds.
- Coagulation stage: Partial_clot (more developed than CH19 but still mostly liquid).
- Density and homogeneity: Predominantly homogeneous liquid; larger, thicker membranous clot than CH19.
- Unique features: Wide folded veil/membrane hugging the bottom and lower sidewall.

Comparisons
- Most advanced coagulation: Control (largest consolidated clot).
- Least advanced coagulation: CH19 (only a thin fibrin film).
- Control vs treated: Control is semi-opaque with a large dense clot; treated samples are clear fluids with only thin membranous fibrin/early clot. Control appears slightly paler; treated are brighter yellow.
- CH19 vs CH21: CH21 shows a thicker, more extensive membrane (more advanced) than the narrower film in CH19.
- Overall visual difference between control and treated: Pronounced.
